chapter_title.ar: باب مَوْلِدِ أَبِي مُحَمَّدٍ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ ( عليه السلام )
chapter_title.en: Chapter 124 –Arrival of Abu Muhammad Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> Bin Ali<sup>asws</sup> (11<sup>th</sup> Imam<sup>asws</sup>)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (162/ 6)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (162/ 6)
text: دَخَلْتُ عَلَى أَبِي مُحَمَّدٍ يَوْماً وَ أَنَا أُرِيدُ أَنْ أَسْأَلَهُ مَا أَصُوغُ بِهِ خَاتَماً أَتَبَرَّكُ بِهِ فَجَلَسْتُ وَ أُنْسِيتُ مَا جِئْتُ لَهُ فَلَمَّا وَدَّعْتُ وَ نَهَضْتُ رَمَى إِلَيَّ بِالْخَاتَمِ فَقَالَ أَرَدْتَ فِضَّةً فَأَعْطَيْنَاكَ خَاتَماً رَبِحْتَ الْفَصَّ وَ الْكِرَاءَ هَنَأَكَ اللَّهُ يَا أَبَا هَاشِمٍ فَقُلْتُ يَا سَيِّدِي أَشْهَدُ أَنَّكَ وَلِيُّ اللَّهِ وَ إِمَامِيَ الَّذِي أَدِينُ اللَّهَ بِطَاعَتِهِ فَقَالَ غَفَرَ اللَّهُ لَكَ يَا أَبَا هَاشِمٍ .

en.hubeali: Is’haq, from Abu Hashim Al Ja’fary who said,

‘I went over to Abu Muhammad<sup>asws</sup> (11<sup>th</sup> Imam<sup>asws</sup>) one day and I wanted to ask him<sup>asws</sup> what I could forge a ring with in order to be Blessed by it. So I sat down and I forgot what I had come to him<sup>asws</sup> for. So when I bade farewell and got up, he flicked over a ring to me, and he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘You wanted silver, so we<sup>asws</sup> give you a (ready made) ring. You profited the silver and the forging (of the ring). May Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Bless you, O Abu Hashim!’. So I said, ‘O my Master<sup>asws</sup>! I hereby testify that you<sup>asws</sup> are the Guardian of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> and my Imam<sup>asws</sup> who Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Made a Religion by his<sup>asws</sup> obedience’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘May Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Forgive you, O Abu Hashim!’’.
en.sarwar: 20. Ishaq has said that narrated to me Muhammad ibn al-Rabi‘ al-Sa’i has said the following. “I debated a man who believed in dualism in Ahwaz them went to Surra man Ra‘a. However, his words were stuck to my heart. I was sitting in front of the door of Ahmad al-Khadib that I saw abu Muhammad (a.s.) coming from the house for public gathering. It was the house wherein on a certain day the Sultan would allow the common people to visit him. The Imam (a.s.) looked at me and made a gesture with his forefinger say One, single and only One. I fainted and fell on my face.”
